meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Antigravity Project Template: Break Through Beginner SaaS Blocks
content.seo_description: Stuck in a tutorial loop? Learn how to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to build professional, portfolio-ready SaaS apps as a student.
content.answer_block: The Antigravity project template eliminates the 'blank canvas' paralysis by providing a battle-tested, production-ready architecture. By pairing this scaffold with Claude Code, you offload the boilerplate and focus on business logic, allowing you to ship functional, scalable SaaS applications that prove your technical competence to future employers.
content.intro:
You’ve watched the tutorials, mastered the syntax, and built the 'To-Do' lists, yet you feel miles away from shipping a real product. The gap between following a video and architecting your own vision is where most students stall. You aren't lacking talent; you are lacking a professional-grade framework that handles the heavy lifting of modern SaaS development.

This case study introduces the Antigravity project template—the definitive shortcut for developers who want to move from 'coding student' to 'SaaS builder.' By integrating this template with Claude Code, you will automate the structural complexity of your application, leaving you free to focus on the unique features that make your portfolio stand out.

steps.title: Initializing the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Establish a robust foundation by cloning the Antigravity template, which includes pre-configured authentication, database ORM, and deployment pipelines.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity base template with Next.js, Supabase, and Tailwind CSS pre-configured.

steps.title: Defining the Domain Model
steps.description: Use Claude Code to translate your product idea into a database schema that fits the Antigravity architecture perfectly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a database schema for a subscription-based task manager including Users, Projects, and Tasks tables with proper relational constraints.

steps.title: Implementing Core Business Logic
steps.description: Focus on your unique value proposition by having Claude generate the backend service layers while you oversee the integration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a service layer function that handles project task creation with validation logic and automatic timestamping.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a service layer for task management based on the existing Prisma schema'

steps.title: Building the UI with Component Libraries
steps.description: Leverage the template's design system to build responsive, accessible interfaces without writing CSS from scratch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a dashboard component that lists user tasks using the Antigravity UI library and Lucide icons.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ui 'Build a responsive task dashboard using Antigravity components'

steps.title: Testing and Deployment Readiness
steps.description: Run automated checks to ensure your code meets the quality standards required for a production-ready portfolio piece.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Run the Antigravity test suite and identify any potential security vulnerabilities or performance bottlenecks.

pro_tips: Treat your project repository as your public resume; commit often with descriptive, conventional-style messages.
Always use the 'dry-run' feature in Claude Code before executing complex file system changes to maintain project integrity.
Customize the Antigravity theme variables early to make the project look uniquely yours, preventing the 'generic clone' aesthetic.
Integrate a logging service like Sentry immediately to demonstrate professional-level debugging skills in your portfolio.
Focus on one 'hero' feature that solves a specific user pain point rather than bloating the app with unnecessary functionality.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is deployed, focus on adding a 'public roadmap' or 'changelog' page to demonstrate your iterative development process to potential employers.
content.conversion_block: Stop building toy apps and start shipping software that matters. Our ecosystem provides the mentorship and tooling you need to bridge the gap from student to professional developer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
